steps.title: Identify the key contrast
steps.content: The passage says discoverers can name a minor planet "but they are not required to do so." The blank must describe an action that is allowed but optional, not mandatory.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean allowed but not required. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — permissible: allowed or permitted — discoverers are allowed to name planets but not obligated to, so naming is permissible. ✓
  • Choice B — infeasible: not possible or practicable — the passage shows naming does happen (1083 Salvia), so it is clearly feasible. ✗
  • Choice C — selective: choosing only some — this describes being choosy, not whether the action itself is allowed or required. ✗
  • Choice D — mandatory: required or compulsory — this contradicts "but they are not required to do so" directly. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
